nameMeanings.name: Lorty
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: North German (also Lörtz): from a shortened form of the personal name Lorenz .

variants.name: Lorenz
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Hans, Alois, Erwin, Juergen, Dieter, Fritz, Gerhard, Guenter, Helmut, Ilse, Klaus.

German, Dutch, and Spanish: from local forms (German Laurenz, Lorenz, Dutch Laurens, Catalan Llorenç) of the Latin personal name Laurentius (see Lawrence ). This surname is also found in Poland, Czechia, France (Alsace and Lorraine), Sweden, and Denmark. Compare Lorance , Lorence , and Lorentz .
Germanized or Americanized form of Polish, Czech, Slovak, Croatian, and Slovenian   Lorenc  .

nameMeanings.name: Henry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: A perennially popular given name, of Continental Germanic origin, from haim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was an Old French name, adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. It has been borne by eight kings of England. Not until the 17th century did the form Henry (as opposed to Harry ) become the standard vernacular form, mainly under the influence of the Latin form Henricus and French Henri.

en.lifeSummary: When Henry Lorty Of Axford was born about 1235, his father, Sir Henry II de Lorty, was 37 and his mother, Sabine Revel, was 34. He had at least 2 sons with Mrs. Henry Lorty. He died in 1321, at the age of 87.
